source_reviews.notes: The official Census NAICS page resolves and provides broad sector context; it is not referenced by a finalized score input.

The one-page Census product list expressly covers coordination of logoed advertising-specialty production and delivery, merchandise demonstrations, display lettering and other residual advertising services under 541890. This supports the disclosed mixed-code and promotional-products working hypothesis.
The ASI release reports a $26.6 billion promotional-products industry and a North American distributor-and-supplier survey basis; it is narrative context, not referenced by a finalized score input.

The release's graphic caption reports 50% AI use among extra-large promotional-products distributors with $5 million or more in annual sales and creative copy as the most common application among AI users. The packet correctly discloses that use is not effective adoption and that the statistic is not full-industry or NAICS-wide.

4imprint identifies itself as a direct marketer of promotional products and reports 2025 revenue of $1,346.8 million, gross profit of $436.0 million and operating profit of $145.2 million. It supports the scaled direct-distribution benchmark, while the authored caveat correctly denies small-target comparability.
HALO's October 22, 2025 announcement confirms acquisition of What's Happening Promotions, a promotional marketing agency.
The September 5, 2025 release confirms BDA's acquisition of Harper + Scott and describes the target as a creative branded-merchandise agency.
Overture's January 9, 2026 announcement confirms acquisition of Pride Products, described as a promotional-products distributor managing merchandise programs.

The report presents Q1 2026 marketing/digital-agency EV/adjusted-EBITDA ranges of 3.0x-4.5x for $1 million-$3 million EBITDA and 4.0x-6.5x for $3 million-$10 million, with retainer percentage and vertical specialization as category drivers. Use for 541890 is an adjacent proxy and is expressly caveated.

publication_caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: 4-digit 5418 is an agency-heavy proxy rather than a clean 541890 staffing census.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The role mix is reinterpreted toward the promo-products dominant-subsegment hypothesis; demonstration/event firms would have lower automation.
inputs.pi_dist: 4imprint is a large direct operator, not a lower-middle-market independent.
inputs.pi_moat: No source in this packet measures account portability for 541890 targets.
inputs.t50_years: The observed 50% figure is only for extra-large distributors and measures use, not effective workflow adoption.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Not a directly observed full-industry or NAICS adoption rate.
inputs.historical_analogs: This is an analogue, not a quantified historical study.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Unobserved estimate; do not treat as a demographic fact.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence of this packet's evidence is not proof of no shortage.
inputs.active_consolidators: Announcements do not show acquisition cadence, target size, or willingness to buy every small distributor.
inputs.buy_mult: No disclosed 541890 lower-middle-market transaction multiple was reopened; the cited category is an adjacent proxy.
inputs.exit_mult: Expansion requires demonstrated retention; recent acquisition announcements disclose no transaction multiples.
inputs.pricing_structure: The full catch-all code includes service models that differ from promo distribution.
summary: Mechanics, exact artifact identity, fresh finalization, memo rendering and arithmetic all pass. All nine registered sources were reopened; the score-driving sources support the limited claims made, and the packet openly treats promotional-products distribution, adoption, role mix and valuation as scoped proxies or estimates. The low-confidence kill thesis remains useful with all 13 authored caveats and no additional material or validator-authored defect.
